Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/3/3 (set-3)

  • Q.P. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

  1. This question paper contains three Sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each Section is compulsory.
  3. Section-A has 6 short answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section-B has 4 short answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section-C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Q. 14 is a case study based problem with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION - A

Question Nos. 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

If the distance of the point (1, 1, 1) from the plane x - y + z + {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 is {tex}\frac{5}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}, find the value(s) of {tex}\lambda{/tex}.

 2
2.

Write the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on {tex}\vec { a },{/tex} where {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }.{/tex}

 2
3.

Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex}

 2
4.

Two cards are drawn successively with replacement from a well shuffled pack of 52 cards. Find the probability distribution of the number of spade cards.

 2
5.

Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}{/tex}

 2
6.

A pair of dice is thrown and the sum of the numbers appearing on the dice is observed to be 7. Find the probability that the number 5 has appeared on atleast one die.

 2

OR

The probability that A hits a target is {tex}\frac 13{/tex} and the probability that B hits it, is {tex}\frac 25{/tex}. What is the probability that the target will be hit, if each one of A and B shoots at the target?

SECTION - B

Question Nos. 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

Find the shortest distance between the following lines : {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+5 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+7 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}  {tex}+\lambda(\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(7 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-6 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex}

 3
8.

The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are represented by vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}. Find the unit vector parallel to one of its diagonals. Also, find the area of the parallelogram.

 3

OR

If {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} 
9.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\mathrm{y}{/tex} = {tex}x^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^x{/tex}, given y(1) = 0.

 3

OR

Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1).
10.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\sin |x|+\cos |x|) d x{/tex}

 3

SECTION - C

Question Nos. 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the distance of the point (1, -2, 9) from the point of intersection of the line {tex}​​\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=4 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+7 \hat{\mathrm{k}}+{/tex} {tex}\lambda(3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} and the plane {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} = 10.

 4
12.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex}.

 4

OR

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_{-2}^1 \sqrt{5-4 x-x^2} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}
13.

Find the area of the region enclosed by the curves y2 = x, x = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}, y = 0 and x = 1, using integration.

 4

Case Study Problem :
14.

A shopkeeper sells three types of flower seeds A1, A2, A3. They are sold in the form of a mixture, where the proportions of these seeds are 4 : 4 : 2, respectively. The germination rates of the three types of seeds are 45%, 60% and 35% respectively.

Based on the above information:

(i)

Calculate the probability that a randomly chosen seed will germinate;

 2
(ii)

Calculate the probability that the seed is of type A2, given that a randomly chosen seed germinates.

 2

Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/3/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 8 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

  1. This question paper contains three Sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each Section is compulsory.
  3. Section-A has 6 short answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section-B has 4 short answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section-C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Q. 14 is a case study based problem with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION - A

Question Nos. 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

If the distance of the point (1, 1, 1) from the plane x - y + z + {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 is {tex}\frac{5}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}, find the value(s) of {tex}\lambda{/tex}.

Solution:

P(1,1,1) and equation of plane x - y + z + {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0
Now, distance between a point and plane
{tex}D=\frac{Ax_1+By_1+Cz_1-D}{\sqrt{A^2+B^2+C^2}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac5{\sqrt3}=\frac{1-1+1+\lambda}{\sqrt{1+1+1}}{/tex}
​​​​​​​{tex}\frac5{\sqrt3}=\frac{1+\lambda}{\sqrt3}{/tex}
​​​​​​​{tex}\lambda=4{/tex}​​​​​​​

 2
2.

Write the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on {tex}\vec { a },{/tex} where {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }.{/tex}

Solution:

 We have to find the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on  {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} 

Given that {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and
{tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }{/tex}
Consider, {tex}( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) = ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } ) + (2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex}
{tex}3 \hat { i} + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k }{/tex}
Now, the projection of {tex}\vec { b } + \vec { c }{/tex} on {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} is given by
{tex}\frac { ( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) \cdot \vec { a } } { | \vec { a } | } ={/tex}{tex}\frac { ( 3 \hat { i } + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } ) } { \sqrt { 2 ^ { 2 } + ( - 2 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 1 ) ^ { 2 } } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 6 - 2 + 2 } { \sqrt { 4 + 4 + 1 } } = \frac { 6 } { 3 } = 2{/tex}

 2
3.

Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3\mathrm e^{2x}+3\mathrm e^{4x}}{\mathrm e^x+\frac1{e^x}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{\left(3\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}+3\mathrm e^{4\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3e^{2x}\left(1+\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=3e^{3x}{/tex}
{tex}\int dy=\int\left(3e^{3x}\right)dx{/tex} (integrating both sides)
{tex}y=3{\textstyle\frac{e^{3x}}3}+C{/tex}
y = e3x + C
This is the general solution.

 2
4.

Two cards are drawn successively with replacement from a well shuffled pack of 52 cards. Find the probability distribution of the number of spade cards.

Solution:

Let X derigte the number of spades in a sample of 2 drawn cards from a well shuffle pack of 52 cards.
then X can take the values 0, 1, 2.
P(X = 0) {tex}\neq{/tex} P(no spade)
{tex}=\frac{{ }^{39} C_2}{{}^{52}C_2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{741}{1326}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{19}{34}{/tex}
P(X = 1) = P(one spade card)
{tex}=\frac{{ }^{13} C_1 \times{ }^{39} C_1}{{ }^{52} C_2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{507}{1326}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{13}{34}{/tex}
P(X = 2) = P(both cards ane spade)
{tex}=\frac{{ }^{13} C_2}{{ }^{52} C_2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{78}{1326}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{17}{/tex}

Thus, the probability distribution of X is given by 

X 0 1 2
f(X) {tex}\frac{19}{34}{/tex} {tex}\frac{13}{34}{/tex} {tex}\frac1{17}{/tex}
2
5.

Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}{/tex}

Solution:

We have x2 - 6x + 13 = x2 - 6x + 32 - 32 + 13 = (x - 3)2 + 4
So, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{1}{(x-3)^{2}+2^{2}} d x{/tex} 
Let x – 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt
Therefore, 
{tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+2^{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x-3}{2}+C{/tex}

 2
6.

A pair of dice is thrown and the sum of the numbers appearing on the dice is observed to be 7. Find the probability that the number 5 has appeared on atleast one die.

Solution:

Let A be the event having sum 7 on the dice
A = (1, 6), (6, 1), (5, 2), (2, 5), (4, 3), (3, 4)
n(A) = 6
Let B be the event in which 5 appears atleast on one die
B = (1, 5), (2, 5), (3, 5), (4, 5), (5, 5), (6, 5), (5, 1), (5, 2), (5, 3), (5, 4), (5, 6)
n(B) = 11
A{tex}\cap{/tex}B = (2, 5), (5, 2)
{tex}P\left(\frac BA\right)=\frac{n\left(A\cap B\right)}{n\left(A\right)}=\frac26=\frac13{/tex}
So, the probability that 5 appears atleast on one dice is {tex}\frac13{/tex}

 2

OR

The probability that A hits a target is {tex}\frac 13{/tex} and the probability that B hits it, is {tex}\frac 25{/tex}. What is the probability that the target will be hit, if each one of A and B shoots at the target?

Solution:

P(A) = P(A hits target) = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
P(B) = P(B hits target) = {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex}
Now, Target will be hit if atleast one of them hits the target. Then, required probability is given by,
P(A {tex}\cup{/tex}B) = P (target will be hit by either A or B)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P(A {tex}\cup{/tex} B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A {tex}\cap{/tex} B)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B){/tex} = P(A) + P(B) - P(A)P(B) [A and B are independent]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{1}{3}+\frac{2}{5}-\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{2}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{5+6-2}{15}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{9}{15}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}

 2

SECTION - B

Question Nos. 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

Find the shortest distance between the following lines : {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+5 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+7 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}  {tex}+\lambda(\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(7 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-6 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex}

Solution:

Given {tex}\overrightarrow r=3\widehat i+5\widehat j+7\widehat k+\lambda\left(\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex}
And {tex}\overrightarrow r=-\widehat i-\widehat j-\widehat k+\mu\left(\widehat{7i}-6\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex}
So a1 = {tex}3\widehat i+5\widehat j+7\widehat k{/tex} , a2 = {tex}-\widehat i-\widehat j-\widehat k{/tex}
And b1 = {tex}\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} , b2 = {tex}7\widehat i-6\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}d=\left|\frac{\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right).\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right|}\right|{/tex}
{tex}\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right)=-4\widehat i-6\widehat j-8\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&-2&1\\7&-6&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} {tex}=4\widehat i-6\widehat j+8\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right|=\sqrt{16+36+64}=\sqrt{116}{/tex}
{tex}d=\left|\frac{\left(-4\widehat i-6\widehat j-8\widehat k\right).\left(4\widehat i-6\widehat j+8\widehat k\right)}{\sqrt{116}}\right|{/tex}
{tex}d=\left|\frac{-16+36-64}{\sqrt{116}}\right|=\frac{44}{\sqrt{116}}{/tex}

 3
8.

The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are represented by vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}. Find the unit vector parallel to one of its diagonals. Also, find the area of the parallelogram.

Solution:

Let {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex}, {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex}

{tex}\overrightarrow c=\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow c=3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\overrightarrow{\left|c\right|}=\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2+\left(6\right)^2+\left(2\right)^2}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex}​​​​​​​
Unit vector {tex}\widehat c=\frac{\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|}=\frac17\;\left(3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k\right){/tex}​​​​​​​
ar(ABCD) = {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\2&-4&5\\1&-2&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\left|\left(12+10\right)\widehat i-\left(-6-6\right)\widehat j+\left(-4+4\right)\widehat k\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\left|22\widehat i+12\widehat j\right|=\sqrt{\left(22\right)^2+\left(12\right)^2}{/tex}​​​​​​
ar (ABCD) = {tex}\sqrt{484+144} = \sqrt{628}{/tex} = 25.05 sq unit

 3

OR

If {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} 

Solution:

Given: {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} 

Now {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k + \lambda \left( { - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k} \right){/tex}=

{tex} = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k - \lambda \hat i + 2\lambda \hat j + \lambda \hat k{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \vec a + \lambda \vec b = \left( {2 - \lambda } \right)\hat i + \left( {2 + 2\lambda } \right)\hat j + \left( {3 + \lambda } \right)\hat k{/tex}

Again, {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j = 3\hat i + \hat j + 0\hat k{/tex}

Since, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} therefore, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right).\vec c = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 6 - 3\lambda + 2 + 2\lambda = 0 \Rightarrow - \lambda + 8 = 0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow - \lambda = - 8 \Rightarrow \lambda = 8{/tex}

 3
9.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\mathrm{y}{/tex} = {tex}x^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^x{/tex}, given y(1) = 0.

Solution:

{tex}x\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\mathrm y=\mathrm x^2.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\frac1x\mathrm y=\mathrm x.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex}
It is form of linear differential equation
So, P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex} , {tex}Q=x.e^x{/tex}
IF= {tex}e^{\int Pdx}=e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex}
General solution
y.(IF) = {tex}\int Q\left(IF\right)dx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.\frac1x=\int x.e^x.\frac1xdx+C{/tex}
{tex}\frac yx=e^x+C{/tex}
y = xex + Cx ...(i)
Put x = 1, and y = 0
0 = e + C
C = e
Putting in eqn (i)
y = xex + xe​​​​​​

Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1).

Solution:

{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1)
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx\left(\log\left({\textstyle\frac yx}\right)+1\right){/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} log a - log b = loga/b)
Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v\left(\log\;v+1\right){/tex}
{tex}\frac{dv}{v\;\log v}=\frac{dx}x{/tex}
Integrating both sides
log (logv) = log x + log C
log (logv) = logxC
log v = Cx
{tex}\log\frac yx{/tex} = Cx

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\sin |x|+\cos |x|) d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \limits_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\sin |x|+\cos |x|) d x{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} I is a even function i.e. f(x) = f(-x)
Using property, {tex}\int_\limits{-a}^af\left(x\right)dx=2\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=2\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi2}\left(\sin\left|x\right|+\cos\left|x\right|\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=2\left[-\cos x+\sin x\right]_0^{\frac\pi2}{/tex}
{tex}I=2\left[-\cos\frac\pi2+\sin\frac\pi2+\cos0-\sin0\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=2\left[0+1+1-0\right]=\;2\times2=4{/tex}
I = 4

Find the distance of the point (1, -2, 9) from the point of intersection of the line {tex}​​\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=4 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+7 \hat{\mathrm{k}}+{/tex} {tex}\lambda(3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} and the plane {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} = 10.

Solution:

Given line is {tex}\vec{r}=(4 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}+7 \hat{k})+\lambda(3 \hat{\imath}+4 \hat{\jmath}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}
Any point on the line is
{tex}(4+3 \lambda, 2+4 \lambda, 7+2 \lambda){/tex}
This point lies on the plane {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{\imath}-\hat{\jmath}+\hat{k})=10{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 + 3{tex}\lambda{/tex} - 2 - 4{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 7 + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 9 = 10 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Point of intersection is (7, 6, 9)

d = {tex}\sqrt{(7-1)^2+(6+2)^2+0}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{36+64}{/tex} = 10

12.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex}.

Solution:

We will solve this integral by partial fraction
Let{tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = (Ax + B) (3x2 + 4) + (Cx + D) (x2 + 1)
= (3A + C)x3 + (3B + D) x2 + (4A + C)x + 4B + D
Equating similar terms, we get,
3A + C = 0, 3B + D = 1, 4A + C = 0, 4B + D = 0
Solving, we get, A = 0, B = -1, C = 0, D = 4
Thus,
I = {tex}\int \frac{-d x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\int \frac{4 d x}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex}
= - tan-1x + {tex}\frac{4}{3} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right)+c{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right){/tex}- tan-1x + c

Case Study Problem :
14.

A shopkeeper sells three types of flower seeds A1, A2, A3. They are sold in the form of a mixture, where the proportions of these seeds are 4 : 4 : 2, respectively. The germination rates of the three types of seeds are 45%, 60% and 35% respectively.

  1. Calculate the probability that a randomly chosen seed will germinate. (2)
  2. Calculate the probability that the seed is of type A2, given that a randomly chosen seed germinates. (2)
Solution:

  1. We have, A1 : A2 : A4 = 4 : 4 : 2
    P(A1) = {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex}, P(A2) = {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex} and P(A3) = {tex}\frac{2}{10}{/tex}
    where A1, A2 and A3 denote the three types of flower seed.
    Let E be the event that a seed germinates and {tex}\bar {E}{/tex} be the event that a seed does not germinate.
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_1}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {45} {100}{/tex}, {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_2}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {60} {100}{/tex}, {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {35} {100}{/tex}
    And {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_1}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {55} {100}{/tex}, {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_2}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {40} {100}{/tex}, {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {65} {100}{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} P(E) = P(A1) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_1}){/tex} + P(A2) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_2}){/tex} + P(A3) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_3}){/tex}
    = {tex}\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{45}{100}+\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{60}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \cdot \frac{35}{100}{/tex}
    = {tex}\frac{180}{1000}+\frac{240}{1000}+\frac{70}{1000}=\frac{490}{1000}{/tex} = 0.49

  2. {tex}P (\frac {A_2} {E}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_2\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_2}})}{\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_1\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_1}})+\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_2\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_2}})+\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_3\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_3}})}{/tex}
    {tex}\frac{\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{60}{100}}{\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{45}{100}+\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{60}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \cdot \frac{35}{100}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{240}{1000}}{\frac{180}{1000}+\frac{240}{1000}+\frac{70}{100}}{/tex} 
    = {tex}\frac{\frac{240}{1000}}{\frac{490}{1000}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac {24} {49}{/tex} = 0.48

Write the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on {tex}\vec { a },{/tex} where {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }.{/tex} Solution: We have to find the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} Given that {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }{/tex} Consider, {tex}( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) = ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } ) + (2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex} {tex}3 \hat { i} + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k }{/tex} Now, the projection of {tex}\vec { b } + \vec { c }{/tex} on {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} is given by {tex}\frac { ( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) \cdot \vec { a } } { | \vec { a } | } ={/tex} {tex}\frac { ( 3 \hat { i } + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } ) } { \sqrt { 2 ^ { 2 } + ( - 2 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 1 ) ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 6 - 2 + 2 } { \sqrt { 4 + 4 + 1 } } = \frac { 6 } { 3 } = 2{/tex} 2 3. Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex} Solution: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3\mathrm e^{2x}+3\mathrm e^{4x}}{\mathrm e^x+\frac1{e^x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{\left(3\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}+3\mathrm e^{4\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3e^{2x}\left(1+\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=3e^{3x}{/tex} {tex}\int dy=\int\left(3e^{3x}\right)dx{/tex} (integrating both sides) {tex}y=3{\textstyle\frac{e^{3x}}3}+C{/tex} y = e 3x + C This is the general solution. 2 4. Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}{/tex} Solution: We have x 2 - 6x + 13 = x 2 - 6x + 3 2 - 3 2 + 13 = (x - 3) 2 + 4 So, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{1}{(x-3)^{2}+2^{2}} d x{/tex} Let x – 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+2^{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x-3}{2}+C{/tex} 2 6. A pair of dice is thrown and the sum of the numbers appearing on the dice is observed to be 7. Find the probability that the number 5 has appeared on atleast one die. Solution: Let A be the event having sum 7 on the dice A = (1, 6), (6, 1), (5, 2), (2, 5), (4, 3), (3, 4) n(A) = 6 Let B be the event in which 5 appears atleast on one die B = (1, 5), (2, 5), (3, 5), (4, 5), (5, 5), (6, 5), (5, 1), (5, 2), (5, 3), (5, 4), (5, 6) n(B) = 11 A {tex}\cap{/tex} B = (2, 5), (5, 2) {tex}P\left(\frac BA\right)=\frac{n\left(A\cap B\right)}{n\left(A\right)}=\frac26=\frac13{/tex} So, the probability that 5 appears atleast on one dice is {tex}\frac13{/tex} 2 OR The probability that A hits a target is {tex}\frac 13{/tex} and the probability that B hits it, is {tex}\frac 25{/tex} . Then, required probability is given by, P(A {tex}\cup{/tex} B) = P (target will be hit by either A or B) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P(A {tex}\cup{/tex} B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A {tex}\cap{/tex} B) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B){/tex} = P(A) + P(B) - P(A)P(B) [A and B are independent] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{1}{3}+\frac{2}{5}-\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{2}{5}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{5+6-2}{15}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{9}{15}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{3}{5}{/tex} 2 SECTION - B Question Nos. 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each. 7. Find the shortest distance between the following lines : {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+5 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+7 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}+\lambda(\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(7 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-6 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} Solution: Given {tex}\overrightarrow r=3\widehat i+5\widehat j+7\widehat k+\lambda\left(\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex} And {tex}\overrightarrow r=-\widehat i-\widehat j-\widehat k+\mu\left(\widehat{7i}-6\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex} So a 1 = {tex}3\widehat i+5\widehat j+7\widehat k{/tex} , a 2 = {tex}-\widehat i-\widehat j-\widehat k{/tex} And b 1 = {tex}\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} , b 2 = {tex}7\widehat i-6\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} {tex}d=\left|\frac{\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right).\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right|}\right|{/tex} {tex}\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right)=-4\widehat i-6\widehat j-8\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&-2&1\\7&-6&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} {tex}=4\widehat i-6\widehat j+8\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right|=\sqrt{16+36+64}=\sqrt{116}{/tex} {tex}d=\left|\frac{\left(-4\widehat i-6\widehat j-8\widehat k\right).\left(4\widehat i-6\widehat j+8\widehat k\right)}{\sqrt{116}}\right|{/tex} {tex}d=\left|\frac{-16+36-64}{\sqrt{116}}\right|=\frac{44}{\sqrt{116}}{/tex} 3 8. The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are represented by vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} . \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}\lambda {/tex} Solution: Given: {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} Now {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k + \lambda \left( { - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k} \right){/tex} = {tex} = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k - \lambda \hat i + 2\lambda \hat j + \lambda \hat k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec a + \lambda \vec b = \left( {2 - \lambda } \right)\hat i + \left( {2 + 2\lambda } \right)\hat j + \left( {3 + \lambda } \right)\hat k{/tex} Again, {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j = 3\hat i + \hat j + 0\hat k{/tex} Since, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} therefore, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right).\vec c = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6 - 3\lambda + 2 + 2\lambda = 0 \Rightarrow - \lambda + 8 = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow - \lambda = - 8 \Rightarrow \lambda = 8{/tex} 3 9. Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\mathrm{y}{/tex} = {tex}x^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^x{/tex} , given y(1) = 0. Solution: We will solve this integral by partial fraction Let {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 = (Ax + B) (3x 2 + 4) + (Cx + D) (x 2 + 1) = (3A + C)x 3 + (3B + D) x 2 + (4A + C)x + 4B + D Equating similar terms, we get, 3A + C = 0, 3B + D = 1, 4A + C = 0, 4B + D = 0 Solving, we get, A = 0, B = -1, C = 0, D = 4 Thus, I = {tex}\int \frac{-d x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\int \frac{4 d x}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} = - tan -1 x + {tex}\frac{4}{3} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right)+c{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right){/tex} - tan -1 x + c 4 OR Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_{-2}^1 \sqrt{5-4 x-x^2} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex} Solution: {tex}I=\int\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x^2+4x+4-4-5\right)}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x+2\right)^2+9}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} As, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac x2\sqrt{a^2-x^2}+\frac{a^2}2\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac xa}+C{/tex} So, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac{x+2}2\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}+\frac92\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac{x+2}3}+C{/tex} 4 13. A shopkeeper sells three types of flower seeds A1, A2, A3. They are sold in the form of a mixture, where the proportions of these seeds are 4 : 4 : 2, respectively. The germination rates of the three types of seeds are 45%, 60% and 35% respectively. Calculate the probability that a randomly chosen seed will germinate. (2) Calculate the probability that the seed is of type A2, given that a randomly chosen seed germinates. (2) Solution: We have, A 1 : A 2 : A 4 = 4 : 4 : 2 P(A1) = {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex} , P(A 2 ) = {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex} and P(A 3 ) = {tex}\frac{2}{10}{/tex} where A 1 , A 2 and A 3 denote the three types of flower seed. Let E be the event that a seed germinates and {tex}\bar {E}{/tex} be the event that a seed does not germinate. {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_1}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {45} {100}{/tex} , {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_2}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {60} {100}{/tex} , {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {35} {100}{/tex} And {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_1}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {55} {100}{/tex} , {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_2}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {40} {100}{/tex} , {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {65} {100}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} P(E) = P(A 1 ) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_1}){/tex} + P(A 2 ) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_2}){/tex} + P(A 3 ) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{45}{100}+\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{60}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \cdot \frac{35}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{180}{1000}+\frac{240}{1000}+\frac{70}{1000}=\frac{490}{1000}{/tex} = 0.49 {tex}P (\frac {A_2} {E}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_2\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_2}})}{\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_1\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_1}})+\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_2\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_2}})+\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_3\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_3}})}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{60}{100}}{\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{45}{100}+\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{60}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \cdot \frac{35}{100}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{240}{1000}}{\frac{180}{1000}+\frac{240}{1000}+\frac{70}{100}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{240}{1000}}{\frac{490}{1000}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac {24} {49}{/tex} = 0.48 4
